metadata.title: Analytical Procedures and Methods Validation for Drugs and Biologics

rules.rule_text: Each NDA and ANDA must include the analytical procedures necessary to ensure the identity, strength, quality, purity, and potency of the drug substance and drug product.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each NDA and ANDA must include the analytical procedures necessary to ensure the identity, strength, quality, purity, and potency of the drug substance and drug product.

rules.rule_text: Each BLA must include a full description of the manufacturing process, including analytical procedures that demonstrate the manufactured product meets prescribed standards of identity, quality, safety, purity, and potency.
rules.verbatim_quote: Each BLA must include a full description of the manufacturing process, including analytical procedures that demonstrate the manufactured product meets prescribed standards of identity, quality, safety, purity, and potency.

rules.rule_text: Data must be available to establish that the analytical procedures used in testing meet proper standards of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility and are suitable for their intended purpose.
rules.verbatim_quote: Data must be available to establish that the analytical procedures used in testing meet proper standards of accuracy, sensitivity, specificity, and reproducibility and are suitable for their intended purpose.

rules.rule_text: Analytical procedures verification or validation data should be submitted in the corresponding sections of the application in the ICH M2 eCTD: Electronic Common Technical Document Specification.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analytical procedures verification or validation data should be submitted in the corresponding sections of the application in the ICH M2 eCTD: Electronic Common Technical Document Specification.

rules.rule_text: You should describe analytical procedures in sufficient detail to allow a competent analyst to reproduce the necessary conditions and obtain results within the proposed acceptance criteria.
rules.source_section: Section IV. Content of Analytical Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: You should describe analytical procedures in sufficient detail to allow a competent analyst to reproduce the necessary conditions and obtain results within the proposed acceptance criteria.
rules.exceptions: An analytical procedure may be referenced from FDA-recognized sources (e.g., USP/NF, AOAC International) if the referenced analytical procedure is not modified beyond what is allowed in the published method.

rules.rule_text: The analytical procedure must include a description of the principle/scope: the basic principles of the analytical test/technology, target analyte(s) and sample(s) type.
rules.verbatim_quote: A description of the basic principles of the analytical test/technology (i.e., separation, detection); target analyte(s) and sample(s) type (e.g., drug substance, drug product, impurities or compounds in biological fluids).

rules.rule_text: The analytical procedure must document all required qualified equipment and components, including instrument type, detector, column type, dimensions, alternative column, and filter type.
rules.verbatim_quote: All required qualified equipment and components (e.g., instrument type, detector, column type, dimensions, and alternative column, filter type).

rules.rule_text: Reagents and standards documentation must include description, grade of chemical, source, purity and state, potencies where required, storage conditions, directions for safe use, and validated or documented shelf life.
rules.verbatim_quote: The following should be listed where applicable: Description of reagent or standard; Grade of chemical (e.g., USP/NF, American Chemical Society, High Performance or Pressure Liquid Chromatography, or Gas Chromatography and preservative-free); Source (e.g., USP reference standard, qualified in-house reference material, WHO International Standard/Reference Material, CBER standard); Purity (for pure chemicals only), State (e.g., dried, undried), and concentration; Potencies (where required by CFR, USP); Storage conditions; Directions for safe use (as per current Safety Data Sheet); Validated or documented shelf life

rules.rule_text: New batches of biological reagents, such as monoclonal antibodies, polyclonal antisera, or cells, may need extensive qualification procedures included as part of the analytical procedure.
rules.verbatim_quote: New batches of biological reagents, such as monoclonal antibodies, polyclonal antisera, or cells, may need extensive qualification procedures included as part of the analytical procedure.

rules.rule_text: The analytical procedure must include system suitability confirmatory test(s) procedures and parameters to ensure that the system will function correctly as an integrated system at the time of use.
rules.verbatim_quote: Confirmatory test(s) procedures and parameters to ensure that the system (equipment, electronics, and analytical operations and controls to be analyzed) will function correctly as an integrated system at the time of use.

rules.rule_text: For chromatographic methods, you should include retention times for identification with reference standard comparison basis, relative retention times (RRTs) for known and unknown impurities, acceptable ranges, and sample results reporting criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: For chromatographic methods, you should include retention times (RTs) for identification with reference standard comparison basis, relative retention times (RRTs) (known and unknown impurities) acceptable ranges and sample results reporting criteria.

rules.rule_text: You should strictly follow storage and usage conditions and handling instructions for reference standards to avoid modifications and contaminations, which could result in additional impurities and inaccurate analysis.
rules.source_section: Section V. Reference Standards and Materials
rules.verbatim_quote: You should strictly follow storage and usage conditions and handling instructions for reference standards to avoid modifications and contaminations, which could result in additional impurities and inaccurate analysis.

rules.rule_text: Information supporting reference standards and materials should include qualification test reports and certificates of analysis (including stability protocols, reports, and relevant known impurity profile information) as applicable.
rules.source_section: Section V. Reference Standards and Materials
rules.verbatim_quote: Information supporting reference standards and materials should include qualification test reports and certificates of analysis (including stability protocols, reports, and relevant known impurity profile information) as applicable.

rules.rule_text: For biological products under BLAs, qualification of subsequent reference standard lots should be included in annual reports.
rules.source_section: Section V. Reference Standards and Materials
rules.verbatim_quote: For biological products under BLAs, qualification of subsequent reference standard lots should be included in annual reports.

rules.rule_text: For certain biological products marketed in the U.S., reference standards authorized by CBER must be used before the product can be released to the market.
rules.source_section: Section V. Reference Standards and Materials
rules.verbatim_quote: For certain biological products marketed in the U.S., reference standards authorized by CBER must be used before the product can be released to the market.

rules.rule_text: For biological reference standards, a two-tiered approach is recommended when qualifying new reference standards to prevent drift in quality attributes, involving a comparison of each new reference standard with a primary reference standard linked to clinical trial material and the current manufacturing process.
rules.source_section: Section V. Reference Standards and Materials
rules.verbatim_quote: For biological reference standards and materials, we recommend that you follow a two-tiered approach when qualifying new reference standards to prevent drift in the quality attributes. A two-tiered approach involves a comparison of each new reference standard with a primary reference standard so that it is linked to clinical trial material and the current manufacturing process.

rules.rule_text: Analytical method validation is the process of demonstrating that an analytical procedure is suitable for its intended purpose. Validation data must be generated under a protocol approved by the sponsor following current good manufacturing practices with predetermined and justified acceptance criteria, using qualified instrumentation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analytical method validation is the process of demonstrating that an analytical procedure is suitable for its intended purpose. The methodology and objective of the analytical procedures should be clearly defined and understood before initiating validation studies. This understanding is obtained from scientifically-based method development and optimization studies. Validation data must be generated under a protocol approved by the sponsor following current good manufacturing practices with the description of methodology of each validation characteristic and predetermined and justified acceptance criteria, using qualified instrumentation.

rules.rule_text: You should include details of the validation studies and results with your application. Protocols for both drug substance and product analytes or mixture of analytes in respective matrices should be developed and executed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Protocols for both drug substance and product analytes or mixture of analytes in respective matrices should be developed and executed. You should include details of the validation studies and results with your application.

rules.rule_text: Typical validation characteristics that must be addressed include: Specificity, Linearity, Accuracy, Precision (repeatability, intermediate precision, and reproducibility), Range, Quantitation limit, and Detection limit.
rules.verbatim_quote: Although not all of the validation characteristics are applicable for all types of tests, typical validation characteristics are: Specificity; Linearity; Accuracy; Precision (repeatability, intermediate precision, and reproducibility); Range; Quantitation limit; Detection limit
rules.exceptions: Not all validation characteristics are applicable for all types of tests.

rules.rule_text: To demonstrate specificity of a stability-indicating test, a combination of challenges should be performed, including samples spiked with target analytes and all known interferences; samples that have undergone various laboratory stress conditions; and actual product samples that are either aged or stored under accelerated temperature and humidity conditions.
rules.verbatim_quote: To demonstrate specificity of a stability-indicating test, a combination of challenges should be performed. Some challenges include the use of samples spiked with target analytes and all known interferences; samples that have undergone various laboratory stress conditions; and actual product samples (produced by the final manufacturing process) that are either aged or have been stored under accelerated temperature and humidity conditions.

rules.rule_text: As the holder of the NDA, ANDA, or BLA, you must: (1) submit the data used to establish that the analytical procedures used in testing meet proper standards of accuracy and reliability, and (2) notify the FDA about each change in each condition established in an approved application beyond the variations already provided for in the application, including changes to analytical procedures and other established controls.
rules.verbatim_quote: As the holder of the NDA, ANDA, or BLA, you must: (1) submit the data used to establish that the analytical procedures used in testing meet proper standards of accuracy and reliability, and (2) notify the FDA about each change in each condition established in an approved application beyond the variations already provided for in the application, including changes to analytical procedures and other established controls.

rules.rule_text: The submitted data should include the results from the robustness evaluation of the method, which is typically conducted during method development or as part of a planned validation study.
rules.verbatim_quote: The submitted data should include the results from the robustness evaluation of the method, which is typically conducted during method development or as part of a planned validation study.

rules.rule_text: The suitability of a compendial analytical procedure (e.g., USP/NF, the Official Methods of Analysis of AOAC International, or other recognized standard references) should be verified under actual conditions of use. Information to demonstrate that USP/NF analytical procedures are suitable for the drug product or drug substance should be included in the submission and generated under a verification protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: The suitability of an analytical procedure (e.g., USP/NF, the Official Methods of Analysis of AOAC International, or other recognized standard references) should be verified under actual conditions of use. Information to demonstrate that USP/NF analytical procedures are suitable for the drug product or drug substance should be included in the submission and generated under a verification protocol.
cross_references: USP General Chapter <1226> Verification of Compendial Procedures

rules.rule_text: The verification protocol for compendial procedures should include: (1) compendial methodology to be verified with predetermined acceptance criteria, and (2) details of the methodology (e.g., suitability of reagents, equipment, components, chromatographic conditions, column, detector type(s), sensitivity of detector signal response, system suitability, sample preparation and stability).
rules.verbatim_quote: The verification protocol should include, but is not limited to: (1) compendial methodology to be verified with predetermined acceptance criteria, and (2) details of the methodology (e.g., suitability of reagent(s), equipment, component(s), chromatographic conditions, column, detector type(s), sensitivity of detector signal response, system suitability, sample preparation and stability).
rules.exceptions: Robustness studies of compendial assays do not need to be included, if methods are followed without deviations.

rules.rule_text: All statistical procedures and parameters used in the analysis of validation data should be based on sound principles and appropriate for the intended evaluation. The data analysis should be assured either by using appropriately validated software or independent verification for correctness.
rules.verbatim_quote: All statistical procedures and parameters used in the analysis of the data should be based on sound principles and appropriate for the intended evaluation... The data analysis should be assured either by using appropriately validated software or independent verification for correctness.

rules.rule_text: When developing chemometric and/or multivariate models for analytical methods, the number of samples to provide adequate statistical power and range for model development and validation should be considered. Suitable software should be used for data analysis. Model parameters should be deliberately varied to test model robustness.
rules.verbatim_quote: Some analytical methods might use chemometric and/or multivariate models. When developing these models, the number of samples to provide adequate statistical power and range for model development and validation should be considered. Suitable software should be used for data analysis. Model parameters should be deliberately varied to test model robustness.

rules.rule_text: Once an analytical procedure is successfully validated or verified and implemented, the procedure should be followed during the life cycle of the product to continually assure that it remains fit for its intended purpose. Trend analysis on method performance should be performed at regular intervals.
rules.source_section: Section VIII. Life Cycle Management of Analytical Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: Once an analytical procedure (including compendial methods) is successfully validated (or verified) and implemented, the procedure should be followed during the life cycle of the product to continually assure that it remains fit for its intended purpose. Trend analysis on method performance should be performed at regular intervals to evaluate the need to optimize the analytical procedure or to revalidate all or a part of the analytical procedure.

rules.rule_text: If an analytical procedure can only meet the established system suitability requirements with repeated adjustments to the operating conditions stated in the analytical procedure, the analytical procedure should be reevaluated, revalidated, or amended, as appropriate.
rules.source_section: Section VIII. Life Cycle Management of Analytical Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: If an analytical procedure can only meet the established system suitability requirements with repeated adjustments to the operating conditions stated in the analytical procedure, the analytical procedure should be reevaluated, revalidated, or amended, as appropriate.

rules.rule_text: In anticipation of life cycle changes in analytics, an appropriate number of retention samples should be maintained to allow for comparative studies. The number should be based on scientific principles and an assessment of risk.
rules.source_section: Section VIII. Life Cycle Management of Analytical Procedures
rules.verbatim_quote: In anticipation of life cycle changes in analytics, an appropriate number of retention samples should be maintained to allow for comparative studies. The number should be based on scientific principles and an assessment of risk.

rules.rule_text: When a change is made to an analytical procedure, revalidation of all or part of the analytical procedure should be considered. Revalidation may also be warranted because of manufacturing process changes that could impact method performance, such as route of synthesis, fermentation, or introduction of a new drug product formulation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Principles described in the validation section (section VI) apply to revalidation. When a change is made to an analytical procedure (e.g., a change in a piece of equipment or reagent or because of a change in manufacturing process or formulation), revalidation of all or part of the analytical procedure should be considered. Analytical method revalidation may also be warranted because of manufacturing process changes, such as an alteration in the drug substance manufacturing process that could impact method performance (e.g., route of synthesis, fermentation) or introduction of a new drug product formulation.
rules.exceptions: The degree of revalidation depends on the nature of the change.

rules.rule_text: For an NDA or ANDA, you should include any proposed alternate analytical procedures in the application. You must include a description of the procedure. After approval, the addition, revision, or deletion of an alternative analytical procedure providing the same or increased assurance must be documented in the next annual report.
rules.verbatim_quote: An alternative analytical procedure is an analytical procedure that you use in place of the FDA-approved analytical procedure. For an NDA or ANDA, you should include any proposed alternate analytical procedures in the application. You must include a description of the procedure. After approval, for an NDA or ANDA, or for a procedure approved in a BLA but not included in an FDA regulation, the addition, revision, or deletion of an alternative analytical procedure that provides the same or increased assurance of the identity, strength, quality, purity, or potency of the material being tested as the analytical procedure described in the approved application, must be documented in the next annual report.

rules.rule_text: For biological products where the analytical method is described in an FDA regulation, you must submit the alternate method for review and approval according to 21 CFR 610.9(a) and present evidence demonstrating that the modification will provide assurances of safety, purity, potency, and effectiveness of the biological product equal to or greater than those provided by the specified method.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the analytical method required is described by a regulation, however, and you want to use an alternate method, you must submit the alternate method for review and approval according to 21 CFR 610.9(a). You must present evidence "…demonstrating that the modification will provide assurances of the safety, purity, potency, and effectiveness of the biological product equal to or greater than the assurances provided by the method or process specified in the general standards or additional standards for the biological product."

rules.rule_text: An analytical method comparability study must demonstrate at a minimum that: (1) the new method coupled with any additional control measures is equivalent or superior to the original method for the intended purpose, and (2) the new analytical procedure is not more susceptible to matrix effects than the original procedure.
rules.verbatim_quote: You should perform an analytical method comparability study that demonstrates at a minimum that: The new method coupled with any additional control measures is equivalent or superior to the original method for the intended purpose. The new analytical procedure is not more susceptible to matrix effects than the original procedure.

rules.rule_text: Postmarketing changes to analytical procedures must be reported to the FDA in compliance with 21 CFR 314.70 or 21 CFR 601.12.
rules.source_section: Section VIII.C. Reporting Postmarketing Changes to an Approved NDA, ANDA, or BLA
rules.verbatim_quote: Postmarketing changes to analytical procedures must be reported to the FDA in compliance with 21 CFR 314.70 or 21 CFR 601.12.

rules.rule_text: Part of the approval process for NDAs and ANDAs may include FDA laboratory assessment to determine whether the analytical procedures are acceptable for quality control and suitable for regulatory purposes. If a laboratory assessment will be conducted, the FDA laboratory will send a request detailing what samples and supplies to send to the FDA laboratory.
rules.verbatim_quote: Part of the approval process for NDAs and ANDAs may include FDA laboratory assessment to determine whether the analytical procedures are acceptable for quality control and suitable for regulatory purposes. If a laboratory assessment will be conducted, the FDA laboratory will send you a request that will detail what samples and supplies to send to the FDA laboratory.
